rationale: The citing paper gives this study a specific role in theory development, not just a background list mention. It identifies Grewal, Chandrashekaran, and Citrin (2010) as one of the few firm-level studies showing an indirect satisfaction -> shareholder value pathway (via sales in airlines), and uses that evidence to argue that mediation should also be tested via loyalty intentions. This citation is also integrated into the paper's structured literature review table and motivates the authors' multilevel mediation design across industries.
relation_description: The citing paper extends Grewal et al. (2010) by building on its indirect satisfaction-shareholder value logic and testing a different mediator (loyalty intentions) in a broader cross-industry, multilevel framework. It generalizes beyond the prior single-industry airline context while preserving the same core line of inquiry about mediated effects on shareholder value.
